verdict: Lead engine is working: cost per lead beats industry, reviews and calls back it up.
diagnosis: Spent $4,356 to generate 165 leads at roughly $26 each, comfortably under the $72 legal-services benchmark, with 10 new five-star reviews reinforcing trust.

signals.headline: 165 leads at about $26 each
signals.detail: Meta lead-gen campaign produced 165 form submissions on $4,356 spend, well below typical legal-vertical costs.

signals.headline: 10 new five-star reviews this period
signals.detail: Google profile now sits at 253 total reviews with a perfect 5.0 average rating, strong social proof for debt-relief seekers.

signals.headline: 129 phone calls from Google profile
signals.detail: Local search drove meaningful inbound calls, the highest-intent action for a debt-relief prospect.

signals.headline: 972 landing page views at $4.48
signals.detail: Roughly 36% of clicks reached the page, healthy for a paid social funnel in a sensitive category.

signals.headline: Instagram following stuck at 28
signals.detail: Organic social is essentially dormant; reach of 4,176 is not converting to follows or profile visits.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.body: Industry trackers put 2026 Meta cost per lead for legal services between $21 and $72, with some reports citing $120+ for competitive markets. Your $26 sits at the efficient end.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.body: Consumer Chapter 7 filings rose 15% year over year in 2025, and summer months typically see elevated debt-relief search volume as households face mid-year credit card balances.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.subheader: Firms shifting to exclusive lead buys
competitor.body: Debt-relief competitors increasingly pay premium prices for exclusive, pre-qualified leads with $20,000+ debt loads. Your in-house Meta funnel is cheaper but needs strong qualification questions to match quality.

recommendations.title: Add qualifying questions to the lead form
recommendations.detail: Consider asking debt amount and timeline inside the Meta form. Filters out tire-kickers and pushes the 165 leads closer to the $20K+ debt profile competitors pay premiums for.

recommendations.title: Test a five-minute callback rule for new leads
recommendations.detail: Debt-relief prospects contact the first firm that answers. Worth testing whether faster intake on the 165 monthly leads lifts retainer conversion without spending more on ads.

recommendations.title: Repurpose five-star reviews into ad creative
recommendations.detail: Ten new five-star reviews this period are unused ad fuel. Test a static or short video ad featuring a real review against the current Static-2 creative.
